input_dict.agent_role: Integrate second-hop evidence with first-hop context and retain the facts needed to answer the question.
input_dict.question: Between Jason Bateman and Jan Troell, which filmmaker has had a more diverse career?
input_dict.current_output: Based on the provided evidence, they are roughly equally diverse: Jan Troell is credited as a director, scriptwriter and director of photography, while Jason Bateman is credited as an actor, director and producer. Jan Troell usually writes his own scripts and serves as his own director of photography. Jason Bateman has extensive TV and film acting credits (specific directing/producing credits are not detailed in the supplied passages).

upstream_context.question: Between Jason Bateman and Jan Troell, which filmmaker has had a more diverse career?
upstream_context.summary_1:
Primary topics: Jan Troell and Jason Bateman — career roles and credits.

- Jan Troell (born 23 July 1931): Swedish film director; passages state he "writes his own scripts" and "serves as his own director of photography"; directed films including "Hurricane" (1979), "Everlasting Moments" (2008), and "Land of Dreams" (1988).
- Jason Bateman (born January 14, 1969): American "actor, director, and producer"; began acting on TV in the early 1980s ("Little House on the Prairie", "Silver Spoons", "The Hogan Family"); credited acting roles include "Arrested Development", many film credits ("Juno", "Horrible Bosses", etc.) and the Netflix series "Ozark".
- Missing bridge fact: comparative measure of "career diversity" — specifically the full set and counts of distinct professional roles (e.g., actor, director, producer, screenwriter, cinematographer), media types worked in (film vs TV vs documentary/essay), and directing/producing credits for each. 
  - Possible bridge entity: Jan Troell — passages show scriptwriting and cinematography work but do not provide a complete credited-roles list or full filmography breakdown.
  - Possible bridge entity: Jason Bateman — passages show acting/directing/producing roles but do not list complete directing/producing credits or other role types (e.g., writing/cinematography).
- Next-hop retrieval directives (priority order): 
  1) "Jan Troell" Wikipedia page — inspect lede/infobox (occupations), "Career" and "Filmography" sections for full credited roles and media types; 
  2) "Jason Bateman" Wikipedia page — inspect lede/infobox (occupations), "Career", "Filmography" (Actor / Director / Producer subsections) and "Awards and nominations"; 
  3) Search/query: "Jan Troell filmography credits cinematography screenwriter" and "Jason Bateman directing credits producing credits" to capture authoritative film databases or filmography lists if needed.
hop2_docs: Hurricane (1979 film) | Hurricane is a 1979 romance and adventure film featuring Mia Farrow and Jason Robards, produced by Dino De Laurentiis and Lorenzo Semple, Jr., and directed by Jan Troell.  It is loosely based on the 1937 film of the same name.

Jan Troell | Jan Gustaf Troell (born 23 July 1931) is a Swedish film director.  Usually, Troell writes his own scripts and serves as his own director of photography.  His realistic films, with a lyrical photography in which nature is prominent, have placed him in the first rank of modern Swedish film directors along with Ingmar Bergman and Bo Widerberg.

Hamsun (film) | Hamsun is a 1996 internationally co-produced drama film directed by Jan Troell, about the later life of the Norwegian author Knut Hamsun (Max von Sydow), who, together with his wife Marie Hamsun (Ghita Nørby), went from being a national hero to a traitor after supporting Nazi Germany during their occupation of Norway during World War II.

Breaking the Rules (film) | Breaking the Rules is a 1992 American drama film directed by Neal Israel, executive produced by Larry A. Thompson, starring Jason Bateman, C. Thomas Howell, Jonathan Silverman and Annie Potts.  Jason's father, Kent Bateman, has a role in the movie as well.
Zandy's Bride | Zandy's Bride is a 1974 American film directed by Jan Troell.  It stars Gene Hackman and Liv Ullmann.

The New Land | The New Land (Swedish: Nybyggarna ) is a 1972 Swedish film co-written and directed by Jan Troell and starring Max von Sydow, Liv Ullmann and Eddie Axberg.  The film is a sequel to Troell's 1971 film "The Emigrants", and both are based on "The Emigrants" novels by Vilhelm Moberg.  Drawing its story from the last two novels, "The Settlers" and "The Last Letter Home", the film is about the Swedish immigrants establishing their home in Minnesota, during the Dakota War of 1862.

Everlasting Moments | Everlasting Moments (Swedish: Maria Larssons eviga ögonblick ) is a 2008 Swedish drama film directed by Jan Troell, starring Maria Heiskanen, Mikael Persbrandt and Jesper Christensen.  It is based on the true story of Maria Larsson, a Swedish working class woman in the early 20th century, who wins a camera in a lottery and goes on to become a photographer.  It has been compared to Troell's previous films "Here's Your Life" and "As White as in Snow", which are both set around the same period.

visible_gold_support.title: Jan Troell
visible_gold_support.sentence: Jan Gustaf Troell (born 23 July 1931) is a Swedish film director.
